title: ICMP and UDP filtering at customer edge causes DoT/DoH fallback loop and intermittent gRPC streaming stalls on private deploy
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent model artifact download failures and GRPC streaming resets from their private deployment. Failures cluster around bulk inference jobs and long-lived streams.

Impact:
- Multiple inference requests returning 502/504 or streaming disconnects for a subset of serving nodes fo...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent model artifact download failures and GRPC streaming resets from their private deployment. Failures cluster around bulk inference jobs and long-lived streams.

Impact:
- Multiple inference requests returning 502/504 or streaming disconnects for a subset of serving nodes for ~10-30s every 5-20 minutes.
- High error rates during model pulls (~10-15% of downloads) when scale-up events occur.
- Customer workload (batch scoring) experiencing timeouts and downstream job failures.

Environment:
- Private deployment (customer-managed VPC) peered with Redwood control plane.
- Edge: Palo Alto SD-WAN appliance + corporate firewall policy.
- Observed region: prod/us-east-1.

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1) Trigger parallel model pulls by scaling serving nodes (e.g., deploy new model or scale replica count).
2) Start long-lived streaming sessio...

title: Timezone-normalized audit stream with deterministic event UUIDs and per-event HMAC for Sumo Logic ingest
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer requests a guaranteed-ordered, timezone-normalized NDJSON audit stream suitable for Sumo Logic HTTP source ingestion. Requirements: deterministic event UUIDs (so identical logical events produce identical ids across retries), per-event HMAC signature header with key-rotation guidance, and UTC...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer requests a guaranteed-ordered, timezone-normalized NDJSON audit stream suitable for Sumo Logic HTTP source ingestion. Requirements: deterministic event UUIDs (so identical logical events produce identical ids across retries), per-event HMAC signature header with key-rotation guidance, and UTC canonicalization of timestamps. This is intended as evidence for an upcoming security audit and to support forensic replayability across their SIEM.

Impact:
- Greenline cannot complete their SIEM onboarding without a reproducible schema and an example ingest configuration.
- They require per-event HMAC verification for tamper-evidence and a signed sample dataset for audit submission.

Environment:
- Customer production account ingest (Greenline-prod) -> Redwood audit webhook -> Sumo Logic HTTP collector (us1.sumologic.com). Region: us-east-1.

Requested deliverables (cust...

title: VPCE HTTP/2 connection coalescing reuses host header causing presigned S3 URL 403s from private deploy
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer FinVault reported intermittent 403 errors when downloading model artifacts from presigned S3 URLs during model initialization in their Private deployment. Errors only occur in production behind the customer's VPC Endpoint (Interface VPCE) and are traced to HTTP/2 connection reuse/coalescing a...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer FinVault reported intermittent 403 errors when downloading model artifacts from presigned S3 URLs during model initialization in their Private deployment. Errors only occur in production behind the customer's VPC Endpoint (Interface VPCE) and are traced to HTTP/2 connection reuse/coalescing at the VPCE layer.

Impact:
- Model pulls for a subset of hosts intermittently fail with HTTP 403 (AccessDenied) despite presigned URL being valid for the request timestamp.
- Failures are non-deterministic: repeated curl downloads may succeed or fail depending on connection reuse.
- Causes failed model pulls during cold starts, resulting in occasional inference errors and longer retries for the customer.

Environment:
- Customer: FinVault Inc. (private deployment)
- Region: us-west-2
- Private control plane with interface VPC Endpoint configured to egress to S3 via a shared...

title: cache-index-mismatch triggers cold KV lookups on repeated prompts causing timeouts
preview: Issue summary: BrightWrite Labs reports repeat prompts (identical user messages sent sequentially) are experiencing significantly higher latencies and occasional 504 timeouts starting 2026-03-12 08:30 UTC. Impact: production traffic for a dedicated tenant; customer reports degraded UX with repeat prompt cases in the...
text: Issue summary: BrightWrite Labs reports repeat prompts (identical user messages sent sequentially) are experiencing significantly higher latencies and occasional 504 timeouts starting 2026-03-12 08:30 UTC. Impact: production traffic for a dedicated tenant; customer reports degraded UX with repeat prompt cases in their chat product, estimated 18% of requests hitting the slow path for recent hour. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated-nyc-1), prod, us-east-1 region. Observed: p50 increased from ~60ms to ~320ms, p95 from ~220ms to ~1.8s for repeat prompts; error spikes of 504/timeouts at ~0.6% during peak. Immediate customer impact: user-facing failures and latency spikes for repeated-turn interactions (e.g., quick clarifications).

title: Cleanup and reconcile SLA orchestration: template variables, routing keys, and reporting parity
preview:
Issue summary:
Recent deploys to sla-enforcer-v2 and the rule-engine refactor created several small divergences between SLA configuration, routing key normalization, and the downstream reporting transforms. The net effect: daily SLA breach counts in the support dashboard are ~7% lower than expected and some internal...
text:
Issue summary:
Recent deploys to sla-enforcer-v2 and the rule-engine refactor created several small divergences between SLA configuration, routing key normalization, and the downstream reporting transforms. The net effect: daily SLA breach counts in the support dashboard are ~7% lower than expected and some internal routing macros do not expand, causing tickets to be auto-assigned to the default queue instead of tiered on-call.

Impact:
- Incorrect auto-assignment for ~120 internal support tickets last week (internal queues).
- Daily SLA breach totals for internal dashboards are undercounted (~7% gap) which affects SRE on-call capacity planning.
- Risk of misrouted P1/P2 escalations if macros are not fixed before next release window.

Observed behavior / Evidence:
- Audit log from sla-enforcer (2026-03-08 03:14 - 03:46 UTC) shows macro expansion failures: variable \"priority_level\" n...

title: WAF header rewrite causing subaccount quota attribution shift and unexpected 429s on private deployment
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightCart (private deployment in eu-west) reported intermittent 429 throttles that started after a WAF policy change rolled out on their edge. The 429s are concentrated on a single subaccount and coincide with high background traffic from a third-party image-optimizer pipeline.

Impact:
- Se...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightCart (private deployment in eu-west) reported intermittent 429 throttles that started after a WAF policy change rolled out on their edge. The 429s are concentrated on a single subaccount and coincide with high background traffic from a third-party image-optimizer pipeline.

Impact:
- Several storefront ingestion workers began receiving 429s between 03:20-04:05 UTC on 2026-03-12, causing delayed catalog updates for ~12% of jobs.
- Customer reports intermittent user-facing slowness for product updates during the window.

Environment:
- Private deployment (customer-managed VPC) running Redwood private control plane (v1.8.3).
- Edge WAF sits in front of the customer ingress and applies custom header rewrites.

Observed behavior:
- Requests that previously attributed usage to distinct subaccounts have been attributed to a single subaccount id (subacct-9812) af...

title: Repeat-prompt latency spike (2-3x) after KV shard-mapping rollout; increased cold reads for prefix lookups
preview: Customer reports 2-3x higher tail latencies for repeated prompt requests in long-running chat sessions starting after 2026-03-09 04:00 UTC. Symptoms match cold KV/prefix lookups: prefix cache hit-rate dropped sharply leading to synchronous remote reads and request timeouts (504) for a subset of requests. Impact: Sev...
text: Customer reports 2-3x higher tail latencies for repeated prompt requests in long-running chat sessions starting after 2026-03-09 04:00 UTC. Symptoms match cold KV/prefix lookups: prefix cache hit-rate dropped sharply leading to synchronous remote reads and request timeouts (504) for a subset of requests. Impact: Several hundred requests/minute for the customer's production org experienced >1s extra latency and intermittent timeouts; affecting their chat product's UX. The customer is on an enterprise Dedicated plan and is running production traffic through our us-east cluster where a KV shard-mapping change was rolled out in the morning of 2026-03-09.

title: Variant redispatch to secondary model during staged canary due to orchestrator snapshot lag
preview: Issue summary: During a staged canary rollout for redwood-llama-7b-v2 in us-east, ~3% of BrightHealth AI traffic was unexpectedly routed to the secondary variant (quantized fallback) despite an explicit tenant model pin. Impact: customer reported mixed responses in production affecting 12 tenant sessions (chat flows...
text:
Issue summary: During a staged canary rollout for redwood-llama-7b-v2 in us-east, ~3% of BrightHealth AI traffic was unexpectedly routed to the secondary variant (quantized fallback) despite an explicit tenant model pin. Impact: customer reported mixed responses in production affecting 12 tenant sessions (chat flows). Symptoms observed at 09:21 UTC: inconsistent model outputs from the same session-id, and increased token latency (p50 -> p95).

High-level hypothesis: orchestrator snapshot application lag caused a partial control-plane commit where edge proxies had stale routing snapshots while the central router had already committed a canary weight update. This led to a redispatch path where requests that should have be pinned were re-evaluated by a reconciliation step and dispatched to the secondary variant.

Requested outcome: restore full pin coherence for affected tenant(s), ident...

title: Runner secret-cache livelock during bulk key rewrap blocks release cutover
preview:
Issue summary:
During a scheduled bulk KMS rewrap for ephemeral runner credentials, a subset of runners entered a secret-cache livelock where vault-sync repeatedly refreshed secrets but the runner-provisioner refused to mark nodes healthy. This prevented the release cutover job from acquiring the required pool of la...
text:
Issue summary:
During a scheduled bulk KMS rewrap for ephemeral runner credentials, a subset of runners entered a secret-cache livelock where vault-sync repeatedly refreshed secrets but the runner-provisioner refused to mark nodes healthy. This prevented the release cutover job from acquiring the required pool of labeled runners and blocked the production deploy window.

Impact:
- Release cutover delayed for two release pipelines (nightly + hotfix).
- ~18% of ephemeral runner fleet in us-west-2 and 12% in eu-central-1 continuously flapped between REGISTERED and UNHEALTHY, failing job assignment.
- Some long-running jobs were evicted and re-queued, creating billing churn.

Environment:
- prod account (redwood-prod) running 2026-03-10 KMS rewrap job initiated from operations-rotate-key pipeline.
- Runner image v2026.02.1, runner-provisioner v1.14.0, vault-sync v0.9.3.
- Autoscaler polic...

title: High attach latency and throughput collapse from small-chunk inference stream CQ saturation
preview: Issue summary: BrightWrite (dedicated pool) reported a mid-morning collapse in steady-state throughput for multiple batched routes. Impact: p50/p95 token throughput drop ~40-70% for routed models and p99 attach latency spiking to 1.2s from baseline ~80ms. Customer-facing inference requests saw elevated 5xx attach ti...
text:
Issue summary: BrightWrite (dedicated pool) reported a mid-morning collapse in steady-state throughput for multiple batched routes. Impact: p50/p95 token throughput drop ~40-70% for routed models and p99 attach latency spiking to 1.2s from baseline ~80ms. Customer-facing inference requests saw elevated 5xx attach timeouts and increased fallbacks to smaller model variants. Environment: prod us-west dedicated pool, heterogeneous GPU hosts (A100 80GB + A10 mixed), customer traffic characterized by many short inference streams (~12-32 tokens) at high qps.

We observed a pattern inconsistent with normal autoscaler signals: autoscaler did not trigger additional hosts despite attach latency rising. Support triaged as potential noisy-tenant + kernel-level IO backpressure (PCIe completion queue saturation) affecting attach path and the serving scheduler fairness.

Impact details: Customer repo...

title: Post-migration edge proxy: intermittent 429s for mixed API keys and signed-url callbacks
preview:
Issue summary:
After a scheduled edge proxy migration (blue->green) on 2026-03-09, the customer reports intermittent 429 responses for a subset of traffic that uses a mix of persistent API keys and signed URL callbacks. Impact: several ETL workers and webhook callbacks saw periodic failures for ~20 minutes during a ...
text:
Issue summary:
After a scheduled edge proxy migration (blue->green) on 2026-03-09, the customer reports intermittent 429 responses for a subset of traffic that uses a mix of persistent API keys and signed URL callbacks. Impact: several ETL workers and webhook callbacks saw periodic failures for ~20 minutes during a heavy ingest window, causing backlog and retries downstream.

Impact:
- Acme Analytics batch ingest pipeline experienced ~18% failed request rate during the window, causing requeues and delayed downstream processing.
- Customer reported billing and operational disruption; they are on a Dedicated plan and requested immediate assistance.

Environment:
- Customer Dedicated pool in us-west-2, routed through new edge-proxy cluster (ep-usw-3).
- Mix of long-lived API keys (service accounts) and short-lived signed URLs generated by their ingestion service.
- HTTP/2 multiplexing be...

title: Batcher synchronization glitch causing periodic p95/p99 latency surges and intermittent 504s for short streaming requests
preview: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours NimbleDocs (dedicated customer) has reported recurring p95/p99 latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts affecting short streaming requests. These occur in bursts approximately every 5-8 minutes and correlate with a rise in batcher flush latency on a subset of nodes. Imp...
text: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours NimbleDocs (dedicated customer) has reported recurring p95/p99 latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts affecting short streaming requests. These occur in bursts approximately every 5-8 minutes and correlate with a rise in batcher flush latency on a subset of nodes. Impact: Customer reports degraded UX for their chat widget during peak hours; some requests hit 504 and are retried by clients. Frequency: spikes observed ~6–12x per hour during the incident window. Initial hypothesis: a synchronization edge-case in the continuous batcher interacting with KV cache warmup/prefetch locks under low-concurrency short-stream traffic.

title: Midday throughput cliff on dedicated capacity traced to silent-tenant heartbeat suppression causing allocator queue blocking and autoscaler stall
preview:
Issue summary:
During a midday traffic window (approx 12:45–13:15 UTC) a FinNova dedicated capacity cluster experienced a sharp drop in successful throughput for batched routes serving redwood-rlhf-13b. P50 remained acceptable but p95/p99 spiked and many requests were queued >10s. Customers reported timeouts and inc...
text:
Issue summary:
During a midday traffic window (approx 12:45–13:15 UTC) a FinNova dedicated capacity cluster experienced a sharp drop in successful throughput for batched routes serving redwood-rlhf-13b. P50 remained acceptable but p95/p99 spiked and many requests were queued >10s. Customers reported timeouts and increased 5xx rate.

Impact:
- FinNova observed ~40% throughput loss for critical batching routes for ~24 minutes.
- Several requests hit route-level timeouts and triggered client retries.
- No billing or data-loss impact observed.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: finnova-prod-1 (reserved)
- Region: us-east-1
- Models: redwood-rlhf-13b (pinned v2026-02-28), fallback to open-llama-13b-v2 enabled

Steps to reproduce (as observed):
1) Targeted load pattern: many small interactive streams (~200–300 simultaneous short streams) started after a quiet period.
2) Autoscaler did not prov...

title: Scheduled alert digest emails strip tracehook query params with leading underscore after retention rollover
preview:
Issue summary: Several customers (first reported by Nimbus Analytics) have reported that scheduled alert digest emails generated right after a retention rollover strip specific tracehook query parameters that begin with an underscore (e.g., _trace_id, _route).

Impact: Digests contain links that no longer include th...
text:
Issue summary: Several customers (first reported by Nimbus Analytics) have reported that scheduled alert digest emails generated right after a retention rollover strip specific tracehook query parameters that begin with an underscore (e.g., _trace_id, _route).

Impact: Digests contain links that no longer include the required tracehook params. When users click the link from the digest the console loads but the trace context is missing, so the alert does not link to the originating trace. This breaks escalation workflows and increases investigation time for on-call engineers.

Environment: Production, runs during nightly retention rollovers and compact windows in us-east.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Have an org with scheduled alert digests configured to include tracehook links (example org: Nimbus Analytics).
2) Ensure retention compaction is scheduled to run within the same hour as the di...

title: Sidecar connection thundering causing reserved-pool slot latch & transient throughput collapse
preview: Issue summary: Between 2026-03-10 12:07 and 12:35 UTC, a customer (DataMuse - dedicated pool) observed a sharp drop in sustained throughput and elevated p99 latency on batched generation routes. Impact: multiple short-lived requests from many clients produced a sidecar connection storm which led to transient GPU slo...
text:
Issue summary: Between 2026-03-10 12:07 and 12:35 UTC, a customer (DataMuse - dedicated pool) observed a sharp drop in sustained throughput and elevated p99 latency on batched generation routes. Impact: multiple short-lived requests from many clients produced a sidecar connection storm which led to transient GPU slot reservations ('slot latch') and prefetch starvation for batched streams; customer reported 40-60% reduction in tokens/sec for 20 minutes. Environment: production reserved pool in us-east; Dedicated capacity cluster: rp-us-east-07; Serving runtime v2.8.1, sidecar-proxy v1.5.3, scheduler v0.9.4.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Customer traffic pattern: thousands of very short streams (median duration ~150ms) originating from a fanout of ~200 client endpoints hitting the same model route.
2) Sidecar proxies opened and closed connections rapidly; many connections reset mid...

title: Mesh routing flap in edge fabric causing extended TLS handshake and request timeouts for us-west customers
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers in the us-west region are observing elevated request latencies and sporadic request timeouts. The symptoms trace to an edge mesh routing flap that produces prolonged TLS handshake duration and occasional connection resets when the gateway picks a cross-region egress path.

Impact:
- Multiple...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers in the us-west region are observing elevated request latencies and sporadic request timeouts. The symptoms trace to an edge mesh routing flap that produces prolonged TLS handshake duration and occasional connection resets when the gateway picks a cross-region egress path.

Impact:
- Multiple Clipstitch Media endpoints experienced 5-10% request failures (timeouts) during the window; p95 increased from ~120ms to ~2.1s for text generation calls.
- Affected features: streaming responses sometimes disconnect mid-stream; non-streaming generation requests hit 504s after backend timeout.

Environment:
- Prod customers in us-west region on Dedicated pools
- API endpoints: /v1/generate, /v1/stream
- Proxy layer: edge-nginx mesh with smart-routing enabled

Observed behavior:
1) Client establishes TCP but TLS handshake time increases unpredictably (handshake durations sam...

title: SAML assertions signed with RSA-PSS rejected by private auth proxy causing 403s for SSO logins
preview:
Issue summary: Customer reports that users in their private deployment cannot complete SSO login. Requests to our auth proxy return 403 Authorization Denied.
Impact: All Aurelia Finance users in the eu-west private deployment are unable to sign in via SSO (affects web console and API token exchange for automation).
...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports that users in their private deployment cannot complete SSO login. Requests to our auth proxy return 403 Authorization Denied.
Impact: All Aurelia Finance users in the eu-west private deployment are unable to sign in via SSO (affects web console and API token exchange for automation).
Environment: Private deployment (customer-managed control plane) running auth-proxy v1.8.1, auth-service v3.2.0. IdP: corporate SAML IdP configured to sign assertions with RSA-PSS (RSASSA-PSS with SHA256).
Observed behaviour: SAML assertion accepted by IdP and delivered to our endpoint, but auth-proxy logs show signature validation failure and downstream RBAC mapping is not performed, resulting in 403.
Expected behaviour: Our SAML verifier should validate IdP-signed assertions and proceed to role mapping; if algorithm unsupported, we should surface clear diagnostic and prov...

title: NVMe disk signature reorder after depot repair causes Intune/assetdb mismatch and Autopilot hostname collision
preview: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames co...
text: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames collide (hostname-1, hostname-1-1), and deployment policies targeted by device ID do not apply. Scope observed: ~7 devices from the same repair batch over the last two weeks. Not observed in fresh builds from imaging lab.
